REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR SEP-09-2024 - CASE SETTLED. ROY ENAV, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MICHAEL SMITH, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT KIA AMERICA, INC. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 610 ON FEB-04-2025 AT 10:30 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385. COURT ISSUES AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO ATTORNEY KEVIN JACOBSON AND WHY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000.00 SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S RULE ON THE NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND TRIAL ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS FAILED TO INFORM THE COURT HABITUALLY WHETHER THE CASE HAS SETTLED OR GIVE AN UPDATE. MISS NIELSEN, COURT STAFF ATTORNEY, HAS LEFT VOICEMAIL MESSAGES ON 9/3/24 AND 9/4/24 AND THERE WAS NO RESPONSE FROM COUNSEL. MR. JACOBSON NEEDS TO APPEAR IN COURT IN PERSON ON 9/23/24 AT 10:00 AM AND EXPLAIN TO THE COURT WHY SANCTIONS SHOULD NOT BE IMPOSED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S RULE. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF JUL-08-2025 IS OFF CALENDAR. DISMISSAL ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar May-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF HUGO ASCENCIO's MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Joshua Kohanoff, Esq., of QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

Michael D. Smith, Esq., of ONGARO PC, (415) 433-3900, for Defendant.

The parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem deciding the subject matter. The parties and Judge Pro Tem (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to execute the formal stipulation on their behalf.

The matter is argued/discussed. Counsel for Defendant requests an extension of the deadline set forth in the court's tentative ruling. The court agrees to extend the deadline to thirty (30) days. No objection to the preceding is presented. Upon conclusion of argument/discussion, the court confirms its tentative ruling, as modified. The unopposed motion is granted as follows: Defendant is ordered to provide Code-compliant responses and all responsive documents to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents, Set Two, Request Nos. 1 through 55, within thirty (30) calendar days from entry of order. Counsel for prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in Word format to the judge pro tem via email to jpdeol@dhillonlaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: John-Paul Deol. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
